title: Abu Dhabi 2-Day Luxury Escape
metaTitle: Abu Dhabi 2-Day Luxury Escape | Flights, Hotels & Tours Included
metaDescription: Book a 2-day trip to Abu Dhabi, United Arab Emirates: round-trip flights, hotel, breakfast, transfers, and guided tours including Louvre Abu Dhabi and Ferrari World Abu Dhabi. Prices from $1420 per person (estimate).
highlights: Overnight luxury desert camp with private butler service
overview: Spend 2 days discovering Abu Dhabi, United Arab Emirates — Desert climate — extremely hot summers (highs 40°C+ and very humid) and warm, pleasant winters (highs around 24-26°C). November to March, for comfortably warm, dry weather and lower humidity than summer. This itinerary blends Louvre Abu Dhabi, Ferrari World Abu Dhabi, Sheikh Zayed Grand Mosque with free time to explore at your own pace, backed by round-trip flights, handpicked accommodation, and a dedicated travel consultant from enquiry through departure.
included: Round-trip economy-class airfare from your selected origin city (upgrade to premium economy or business available)
1 night accommodation in your selected category (Budget, Mid-range, Premium, or Luxury)
Daily local transport as per the itinerary
Guided tours including Louvre Abu Dhabi, Ferrari World Abu Dhabi, Sheikh Zayed Grand Mosque
All entrance fees for attractions and activities listed in the itinerary
Applicable taxes and OTA service fee
Dedicated travel consultant support from booking through departure
notIncluded: Visa fees and travel document costs (see Visa Information)
Travel insurance (strongly recommended, available as an add-on)
Lunches and dinners except where noted in the itinerary
Optional activities not listed in the day-by-day itinerary
Airline seat selection and baggage upgrades
Any fare difference for premium economy or business class upgrades

itinerary.title: Arrive in Abu Dhabi
itinerary.description: Land at Zayed International Airport (AUH) and take your included private transfer to your hotel. With limited time on the ground, head straight out to Louvre Abu Dhabi — Jean Nouvel-designed museum spanning global art and civilization. Round off the evening with a welcome dinner introducing you to local flavors.

itinerary.title: Abu Dhabi Highlights & Departure
itinerary.description: Start early with a visit to Ferrari World Abu Dhabi — Indoor Ferrari-branded theme park including the world's fastest roller coaster. Grab a last taste of local life before your private transfer to AUH for your onward flight.

travelTips: Visit the Sheikh Zayed Grand Mosque in the morning for the best light and smaller crowds — dress code is strictly enforced (abayas provided for visitors if needed).
Book Ferrari World and Yas Island attractions online in advance for better pricing.
Summer heat (June-September) makes outdoor sightseeing impractical outside early morning or evening.
A day trip to Dubai is easy via a 90-minute taxi or bus ride.
With a short stay, book your must-see attractions in advance to avoid losing time to queues.
packingList: Copies of booking confirmations and travel insurance
Lightweight, breathable clothing and a reusable water bottle
Modest clothing covering shoulders and knees for mosques and public areas
Lightweight, breathable clothing for the heat
A light layer for heavily air-conditioned malls and museums
thingsToAvoid: Wearing revealing clothing outside resort pool/beach areas.
Scheduling extensive outdoor activities during summer midday heat.
Photographing government buildings or military installations without checking if it's permitted.
instagramSpots: Louvre Abu Dhabi's dome of light
hiddenGems: Al Ain oasis city day trip
luxuryExperiences: Overnight luxury desert camp with private butler service
Private yacht charter around the Corniche
honeymoonExperiences: Sunset desert safari with private dinner under the stars
Couples spa day at a Corniche resort
Private evening tour of the illuminated Grand Mosque
budgetExperiences: Free visit to the Sheikh Zayed Grand Mosque
localEtiquette: Dress modestly in public areas, especially malls and government buildings — shoulders and knees covered.
Public displays of affection are frowned upon; alcohol is only served in licensed venues.
During Ramadan, avoid eating, drinking, or smoking in public during daylight hours.
Friday is the start of the weekend; some businesses close or shorten hours around Friday prayers.
localTransportGuide: Abu Dhabi is spread out and largely car-oriented — taxis and rideshare apps (Careem, Uber) are inexpensive and the easiest way around. A public bus network exists but is less convenient for visitors; most attractions on Yas and Saadiyat Islands are best reached by taxi.
bestTimeToVisit: November to March, for comfortably warm, dry weather and lower humidity than summer.
weather: Desert climate — extremely hot summers (highs 40°C+ and very humid) and warm, pleasant winters (highs around 24-26°C).
visaInformation: Visa-on-arrival or visa-free entry for many nationalities including the US, UK, EU, Canada, Australia, and India (visa-on-arrival for eligible Indian passport holders meeting conditions, or a pre-arranged e-visa) for stays typically up to 30-90 days depending on nationality.
averageFlightTime: ~9.4h from major hubs (estimate; varies by origin — see Suggested Flights)
cancellationPolicy: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.
termsAndConditions: Pricing shown is per person based on the traveler type and tier selected and is an estimate pending live fare and rate confirmation.
Passports must be valid for at least 6 months beyond the return date for most destinations — confirm the exact requirement for your nationality.
Itinerary order may change due to weather, local conditions, or attraction closures; inclusions remain equivalent.
Solo travelers incur a single-room supplement, reflected in the Solo pricing tier.
This package does not include international travel insurance unless explicitly listed under Included.
Prices shown in a currency other than USD are approximate conversions at the rate noted and are not a payment quote.

faqs.question: Do I need a visa to visit Abu Dhabi?
faqs.answer: Visa-on-arrival or visa-free entry for many nationalities including the US, UK, EU, Canada, Australia, and India (visa-on-arrival for eligible Indian passport holders meeting conditions, or a pre-arranged e-visa) for stays typically up to 30-90 days depending on nationality.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit Abu Dhabi?
faqs.answer: November to March, for comfortably warm, dry weather and lower humidity than summer. Desert climate — extremely hot summers (highs 40°C+ and very humid) and warm, pleasant winters (highs around 24-26°C).

faqs.question: What's included in this package?
faqs.answer: Round-trip airfare, accommodation, daily breakfast, airport transfers, daily local transport, and guided tours to the attractions listed in the itinerary. See the Included / Not Included sections for full detail.

faqs.question: Can the itinerary be customized?
faqs.answer: Yes — this itinerary is a starting point. Your travel consultant can add, remove, or reorder activities, extend your stay, or upgrade your hotel category before you book.

faqs.question: What is the cancellation policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.

faqs.question: Is Abu Dhabi safe for tourists?
faqs.answer: Abu Dhabi is rated very safe for tourists, with low rates of violent crime. Usual travel precautions (watch belongings in crowds, use licensed taxis) still apply.

faqs.question: What currency should I bring to Abu Dhabi?
faqs.answer: The local currency is AED. Cards and mobile payments are widely accepted, and ATMs are easy to find.

faqs.question: Is this package good for a honeymoon or family trip?
faqs.answer: It also works well for families — highlights include Ferrari World.

reviews.author: James Novak
reviews.title: Luxury without the hassle
reviews.body: Our trip to Abu Dhabi was fantastic from start to finish. The hotel was clean and well located, and the guided visit to Louvre Abu Dhabi was a real highlight. Airport transfers were on time both ways.

reviews.author: Nadia Fernandez
reviews.title: A little rushed but still wonderful
reviews.body: Abu Dhabi is stunning and this package covered the essentials well, especially Desert safari with dune bashing. The hotel could have been a little closer to the center but transport was easy.

reviews.author: Priya Larsen
reviews.title: Smooth from start to finish
reviews.body: Booked this as a family of 4 trip and it was well paced. Ferrari World Abu Dhabi in particular stood out. A couple of the transfers ran a little late but nothing that spoiled the trip.

reviews.author: Olivia Ibrahim
reviews.title: Unforgettable trip!
reviews.body: Our trip to Abu Dhabi was fantastic from start to finish. The hotel was clean and well located, and the guided visit to Desert safari with dune bashing was a real highlight. Airport transfers were on time both ways.

reviews.author: Wei Johnson
reviews.title: Worth every penny
reviews.body: Booked this as a solo trip and it was well paced. Founder's Memorial in particular stood out. A couple of the transfers ran a little late but nothing that spoiled the trip.

reviews.author: Elena Santos
reviews.title: Adventure of a lifetime
reviews.body: We added a couple of optional activities beyond Louvre Abu Dhabi and don't regret it. Good value for what was included, and the food recommendations from our guide were spot on.

reviews.author: Mia Novak
reviews.title: A little rushed but still wonderful
reviews.body: Everything was handled for us — flights, hotel, transfers, and tours to Corniche Beach and promenade and Ferrari World Abu Dhabi. Great communication from the consultant before departure too.

reviews.author: Daniel Khan
reviews.title: Luxury without the hassle
reviews.body: Everything was handled for us — flights, hotel, transfers, and tours to Qasr Al Watan (Presidential Palace) and Sheikh Zayed Grand Mosque. Great communication from the consultant before departure too.

reviews.author: Ava Khan
reviews.title: Great value for money
reviews.body: Ferrari World Abu Dhabi alone was worth the trip, but combined with Desert safari with dune bashing this itinerary really delivered. Breakfast at the hotel was good every day and the local guide was knowledgeable.
